title: mfa — subprocessors
description: MFA has NO subprocessors of its own — every third-party integration required for MFA is transitively via identity/identity (KMS / Vault for secret encryption) or via the browser + user's authenticator (for WebAuthn attestation, which is an on-device operation). The WebAuthn spec is designed so that no subprocessor sees the private key — all cryptography is authenticator-local.

transitive_subprocessors.name: AWS KMS / HashiCorp Vault
transitive_subprocessors.purpose: TOTP secret envelope encryption. Delegated to identity/identity's MfaSecretEncryptor — see identity/subprocessors.json for the full contract.
transitive_subprocessors.when_engaged: Every TOTP enrolment confirmation + every TOTP challenge verification (transparent decrypt).
transitive_subprocessors.notes: MFA never invokes KMS / Vault directly; it goes through identity/identity's MfaSecretEncryptor service which owns the credential + endpoint.

on_device_operations.name: WebAuthn authenticator (device-local)
on_device_operations.purpose: Private-key storage + assertion signing lives on the user's authenticator device (Touch ID's Secure Enclave, Windows Hello's TPM, a YubiKey, Android's StrongBox). The private key NEVER leaves the device — the WebAuthn spec is designed so that even the browser can't extract it.
data_processed: Challenge nonce (random bytes we sent) is signed inside the authenticator
on_device_operations.location: user's physical device (phone, laptop, hardware key)
on_device_operations.legal_basis: not a subprocessor — the user's own device is not third-party processing
on_device_operations.notes: The WebAuthn spec is intentionally designed to avoid any 'authenticator vendor as subprocessor' relationship. Apple / Microsoft / Google / Yubico do not receive our authentication challenges; the user's local authenticator does.

not_a_subprocessor: google2fa (RFC 6238 TOTP library) — in-process library, no data leaves the app
web-auth/webauthn-lib (WebAuthn attestation + assertion verifier) — in-process library, no data leaves the app
Argon2id / bcrypt (recovery-code hashing) — in-process; delegated to identity/identity's CredentialHasher
OpenTelemetry / metrics collector — receives aggregate metrics only (no identifiers, no credential material — per metrics.json)
notes: The WebAuthn design specifically avoids the biggest MFA subprocessor risk of the previous decade: SMS-based OTP. We do NOT ship SMS-based MFA and have no intention to — the industry consensus post-NIST-800-63B is that SMS is unfit for MFA due to SIM-swap attacks + carrier compromise. WebAuthn passkeys + TOTP + hardware keys are our answer.

The FIDO Alliance Metadata Service (MDS) is a public read-only registry of authenticator metadata (AAGUID → model, security certifications). If a tenant enables `mfa.webauthn.attestation_conveyance='direct'` + config('mfa.webauthn.require_mds_verified'), the app fetches MDS entries at authenticator-registration time to verify the AAGUID is on the certified list. MDS is a subprocessor for the specific tenants who opt in — it receives (aggregated) fetch counts of MDS metadata but no per-user data. Not enabled by default in Wave 1a.
